releases.title: v3.3.0
releases.summary: This minor release adds a new library loans workflow so borrowed items can be tracked from one management surface instead of disappearing into title-level notes. The release introduces scoped loan records, item-attached loan actions, a dedicated `Loans` view, and stronger current-loan visibility for active, overdue, and returned items across both platform and homelab stacks.

details.heading: Loans workflow foundation
bullets: Added a dedicated `media_loans` record shape with one-active-loan-per-media protection.
Added scoped create, edit, list, and return APIs for loan lifecycle management.
Added OpenAPI coverage for the new loans endpoints.
Normalized loan date fields in API responses to a cleaner date-only contract.

details.heading: Library loans management view
bullets: Added a dedicated `Loans` navigation surface in the library shell.
Added active, overdue, returned, and all-loans filters.
Added edit and return actions directly from the loans view.
Added counts and urgency cues so the screen reads as the place to manage what is currently out.

details.heading: Item-attached loan actions
bullets: Added a loan creation form and active-loan summary inside the media detail drawer.
Added recent loan history visibility for individual titles.
Kept loan entry attached to the title while still making the overall workflow browsable from one place.

details.heading: Verification and release evidence
bullets: Added a Docker-backed loans workflow smoke proving create, update, return, and history behavior.
Verified init parity and migration rehearsal after adding the loans schema.
Regenerated the in-app release feed for `Help > Releases`.

releases.title: v3.2.10
releases.summary: This patch release makes comic provider re-enrichment easier to scan by adding compact cover thumbnails directly in the edit drawer lookup results. The lookup rows now reuse the provider image data already returned by Metron and other search flows, keep the result list compact, and fall back cleanly when a result has no image.

details.heading: Compact lookup thumbnails
bullets: Added a compact thumbnail treatment to provider lookup results in the edit drawer overlay.
Reused the existing image fields already present in lookup payloads instead of introducing any new provider or API contract.
Tightened the thumbnail corners to a more deliberate utility treatment so the results feel functional rather than soft or card-like.

details.heading: Graceful no-image fallback
bullets: Added a lightweight fallback tile for results that do not include an image.
Kept the current title, source badges, subtitle details, and `Apply` action behavior intact.

details.heading: Regression coverage
bullets: Added source-backed regression coverage proving the lookup overlay still includes the compact thumbnail rendering contract.
Rebuilt the platform and homelab frontend bundles so the shipped app includes the new result-row treatment.

releases.title: v3.2.9
releases.summary: This patch release fixes a comic edit and provider-enrichment failure in the Metron flow. When a reselection from Metron returns an oversized description, the app now clamps that overview safely instead of failing save validation, while keeping the field editable in the drawer and preserving deterministic backend behavior for direct API clients.

details.heading: Backend-safe overview normalization
bullets: Added overview normalization in request validation so incoming overview text is trimmed, empty values resolve to `null`, and oversized descriptions are truncated deterministically to the current 10,000-character limit before validation completes.
Kept the existing save contract stable while making the API resilient to provider payloads that exceed the local overview cap.

details.heading: Drawer-side truncation and edit usability
bullets: Clamped provider overview text in the comic edit drawer before save so the common Metron re-enrichment path no longer surprises the user with a validation failure.
Kept the overview field editable and added a compact character count so the drawer remains usable when provider text is long.
Added a lightweight warning when provider overview text had to be truncated to fit the save limit.

details.heading: Docker-backed Metron regression proof
bullets: Added a Docker-backed fake-Metron smoke covering the exact Alpha Flight-style repro: existing comic, Metron reselect, oversized provider overview, successful save, and preserved provider issue identity.
Proved on both platform and homelab stacks that an oversized Metron overview now saves with deterministic truncation instead of failing validation.

releases.title: v3.2.8
releases.summary: This patch release removes the comic-library full-fetch exception from the main browsing paths. Comic issues, comic series summaries, and selected-series issue browsing now all run through server-backed pagination and stable comic ordering contracts built from existing series, issue, and volume metadata instead of browser-only full-list sorting.

details.heading: Server-backed comic issue ordering
bullets: Added a backend `comic_issue` ordering contract built from existing comic metadata with title fallback parsing.
Switched the default comic `issues` tab to use normal paged API results instead of forcing a one-shot full fetch into browser memory.

details.heading: Server-backed comic series summaries
bullets: Added a grouped `/api/media/comic-series` API for paginated comic series summaries.
Switched the comic `series` tab to use grouped server summaries with stable counts, poster selection, and paging.

details.heading: Server-backed selected-series issue browsing
bullets: Added `/api/media/comic-series/issues` for paginated issue browsing inside a selected comic series.
Switched the `series_issues` tab off the last comic full-fetch path so selected-series issue browsing now uses server results with stable in-series ordering.
Added Docker-backed proof coverage for all three comic query contracts across both platform and homelab stacks.

releases.title: v3.2.7
releases.summary: This patch release hardens duplicate prevention for the re-enabled OPDS / digital-library import path. It proves repeat OPDS syncs reuse canonicals safely, fixes the stored OPDS link contract for browse versus download actions, and closes a comic-heavy import gap so parsed comic identity metadata survives persistence without recreating duplicate rows on later syncs.

details.heading: OPDS importer reactivation and repeat-sync proof
bullets: Re-enabled the live `/api/media/import-cwa` route instead of leaving the existing importer stranded behind a deferred response.
Added a Docker-backed repeat-sync smoke proving one OPDS entry imported twice updates or no-ops the same canonical row through persisted `provider_item_id` / `calibre_entry_id` identities.

details.heading: OPDS link contract cleanup
bullets: Separated OPDS browse/detail links from acquisition/download links in the importer contract.
Stopped treating OPDS external URLs as `tmdb_url` stand-ins for books and comics.
Preserved download-link fields through generic import mapping and drawer rendering so Calibre/OPDS actions stay truthful and deduped in the UI.

details.heading: Comic-heavy dedupe hardening
bullets: Added a Docker-backed comic-heavy OPDS smoke that proves a clearly comic-shaped OPDS entry imports as `comic_book`, persists parsed `series`, `issue_number`, and `volume`, and then reuses the same canonical row on repeat sync.
Fixed generic import mapping so row-backed OPDS `type_details` fields are not dropped before persistence, which previously stripped comic identity metadata from live OPDS imports.

releases.title: v3.2.6
releases.summary: This patch release hardens collection-side duplicate prevention after collection merges. It proves that a merged collection can survive a later boxed-set CSV import without recreating the absorbed duplicate container, and it fixes the collection identity persistence needed to make that behavior durable in runtime.

details.heading: Collection import alias durability
bullets: Added collection-side absorbed identity preservation for merged collections through `import_collection_aliases`.
Taught collection-only import lookup to reuse those stored aliases instead of relying only on the current `source_title + import_source + media_type + scope` key.
Fixed alias normalization so stored snake_case collection alias fields survive dedupe and matching correctly.

details.heading: Collection re-sync runtime proof
bullets: Added a Docker-backed runtime smoke that:

releases.title: v3.2.5
releases.summary: This patch release hardens post-merge duplicate avoidance across changing provider contracts and degraded sync payloads. It proves that a canonical created by one source family can be reused by a later Plex sync through TMDB identity, and that a metadata-poor follow-up payload can still resolve through preserved merge aliases instead of recreating a duplicate. The release also fixes a real metadata-clobbering bug exposed by the new sparse alias proof.

details.heading: Cross-provider canonical reuse
bullets: Added runtime proof that a movie canonical first created by a non-Plex source is reused by a later Plex sync through `tmdb_id`.
Proved the reused row keeps its seeded title variant, showing the update happened through strong identity rather than weak title fallback.

details.heading: Sparse alias reuse and metadata preservation
bullets: Added runtime proof that a metadata-poor follow-up CSV payload still resolves to the canonical row through a preserved `provider_item_id` alias after merge.
Fixed imported media updates so sparse `type_details` merge into the existing canonical metadata instead of replacing richer fields wholesale.
Proved richer canonical metadata such as author and ISBN survive the degraded follow-up payload.

releases.title: v3.2.4
releases.summary: This patch release hardens duplicate-avoidance safety during later imports and syncs. It proves that when strong identifiers materially conflict, the system refuses to mutate an existing merged canonical even if weaker title or year signals look similar. The release covers both generic CSV and Plex movie paths, and it fixes a real null-safety issue exposed while expanding the guard proof matrix.

details.heading: Strong-identifier conflict guards
bullets: Added runtime proof that a same-title book import with a conflicting ISBN creates a separate row instead of mutating an existing merged canonical.
Added runtime proof that a same-title movie import with a conflicting UPC creates a separate row instead of mutating an existing merged canonical.
Added runtime proof that a same-title Plex movie import with a conflicting TMDB id creates a separate row instead of mutating an existing merged canonical.

details.heading: Import-path hardening
bullets: Extended the title-fallback import guard logic so strong-identifier conflicts are checked before reusing same-title candidates.
Hardened the Plex movie import fallback so it no longer trusts title/year alone when TMDB or UPC conflicts make reuse unsafe.
Fixed a null-safe incoming strong-identifier helper bug surfaced by the new movie-shaped guard coverage.

releases.title: v3.2.3
releases.summary: This patch release hardens merge durability boundaries. It adds a Docker-backed proof that preserved merge identities only resolve inside the currently active library scope, so later re-syncs cannot silently update or recreate rows in the wrong library when the same external/provider identifier exists elsewhere.

details.heading: Scope-isolated merge re-sync proof
bullets: Added a dedicated runtime smoke for:
Proved the live CSV sync path updates the correct row with:

details.heading: Smoke harness hardening
bullets: Refactored the new scope-isolation smoke to use:
so the proof exercises actual route-level scope rules instead of getting tangled in admin support-session restrictions.

releases.title: v3.2.2
releases.summary: This patch release hardens release readiness evidence. It adds a repo-native local preflight helper that generates dependency audit artifacts and a go/no-go report from live stack evidence, and it remediates the `follow-redirects` dependency advisory in both backend and frontend package trees so the local dependency audit closes cleanly before release.

details.heading: Release evidence hardening
bullets: Added a local release preflight helper that generates:
Made the local preflight explicitly distinguish:
Aligned the release checklist docs with the new preflight workflow.

details.heading: Dependency remediation
bullets: Added package overrides so backend and frontend resolve `follow-redirects` to `1.16.0`.
Refreshed both lockfiles so local dependency audits no longer report the previously tracked moderate advisory.
